cross_scene_arcs.title: The Guardian of the Group
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Benny evolves from a hesitant friend into the emotional and moral protector of the group, consistently placing others' needs above his own. He initiates Scotty's inclusion, defends Yeah-Yeah with protective gear, and ultimately risks his life to retrieve the ball and shield Scotty from consequences.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Benny overcomes personal hesitation to invite Scotty to play, marking the beginning of his role as an inclusive leader.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Benny emerges as a legend after confronting the Beast, having fully embraced his role as the group's guardian through physical and emotional sacrifice.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The psychological toll of his near-death experience and the long-term impact of his protective role on his own identity remain unprocessed.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Leader to Legend
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Benny's status within the group transforms from respected peer to mythologized figure, shaped by his past choices, demonstrated expertise, supernatural validation, and heroic actions. The group increasingly sees him as fated for greatness, culminating in his solitary presence as the enduring symbol of the Sandlot.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Benny is established as a loyal friend who once sacrificed personal advancement for group unity.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Benny becomes the legendary 'Benny "The Jet" Rodriguez,' the last keeper of the Sandlot's mythos, revered as its heart and soul.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Benny downplays his mythic status, creating a tension between the group's veneration and his personal humility.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Hero's Trial and Triumph
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Benny accepts a supernatural call to heroism, embarks on a dangerous quest to retrieve the baseball, survives a life-threatening confrontation with the Beast, and ultimately fulfills his destiny with a legendary steal of home, proving his courage and skill on both a personal and public stage.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Benny is paralyzed by guilt and fear until visited by the ghost of Babe Ruth, who reframes the ball retrieval as his 'big chance.'
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Benny completes his heroic arc by stealing home in a pennant-deciding game, achieving legendary status in the real world as well as in memory.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Cost of Unity
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Benny repeatedly navigates internal and external conflicts to preserve group cohesion, from defending Scotty's inclusion to managing the fallout of the forged signature and ultimately sacrificing a priceless artifact to protect Scotty and maintain peace. Each decision reinforces unity but extracts a personal or symbolic cost.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Benny faces peer resistance when advocating for Scotty's inclusion, risking group harmony to uphold fairness.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Benny resolves the crisis by giving Scotty the 'Murderers' Row' ball, preserving the group's bond at the expense of a historically significant object.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The long-term emotional impact of losing the 'Murderers' Row' ball and whether such sacrifices can continue without eroding individual needs remains unaddressed.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Outsider to Teammate: Scotty's Quest for Belonging
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Scotty evolves from a socially isolated newcomer mocked for his ignorance into a validated member of the baseball group through persistent effort, skill development, and peer recognition.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Scotty is publicly humiliated and socially excluded due to his unawareness of group norms.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Scotty earns full acceptance by hitting a pitch in a real game, symbolizing his transformation into a legitimate team member.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Fractured Bond: Scotty's Struggle for Paternal Connection
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Scotty's repeated attempts to connect with Bill are met with emotional detachment and indifference, culminating in a symbolic restoration of their relationship through mutual play and affirmation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Scotty initiates vulnerable overtures toward Bill, seeking paternal guidance and approval.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Bill fully embraces his role as a father, sharing a joyful moment of catch that heals their emotional rift.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Weight of Deception: Moral Growth and Redemption
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: After covering up the loss of a treasured heirloom, Scotty confronts the moral consequences of his actions, leading him to lead a high-stakes mission and ultimately redeem himself through courage and compassion.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Scotty replaces the stolen Babe Ruth ball with a fake, attempting to avoid punishment and exposure.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Scotty redeems himself by freeing the Beast and restoring trust with both the group and Mr. Mertle, resolving the crisis he helped create.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Outsider Within: Navigating Cultural and Social Codes
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Despite growing acceptance, Scotty remains culturally out of step with the group, repeatedly exposed as unfamiliar with shared rituals and baseball lore, highlighting his ongoing journey of assimilation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Scotty receives a subtle nod from Benny—the first sign of recognition—but remains physically and socially distant.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: The group includes Scotty in rituals like making s'mores despite his confusion, indicating inclusion even as differences persist.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Scotty continues to mask his lack of familiarity with group norms, suggesting his integration is still fragile and performative.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Fear to Courage: Confronting the Unknown
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Spurred by curiosity and responsibility, Scotty transitions from passive observer of the Beast myth to active investigator and savior, transforming fear into heroic action.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Scotty begins investigating the truth behind The Beast after witnessing strange evidence in Mr. Mertle's yard.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Scotty physically frees The Beast, ending its suffering and dispelling the myth, becoming a hero through empathy and bravery.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Keeper of Lore: From Statistician to Mythmaker
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Squints evolves from a baseball statistician into the group's primary mythmaker and cultural authority, using knowledge and storytelling to shape group identity and belief.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Squints is recognized for his baseball knowledge and role in celebrating major league milestones with the group.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Squints is revered as the spiritual and narrative leader of the group, interpreting omens and shaping belief through ritual and myth.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: His influence relies on belief rather than proof, leaving his authority vulnerable to skepticism or shifting group dynamics.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Mockery to Empathy: Squints and the Ethics of Belonging
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Squints navigates the tension between enforcing group norms and confronting his own hypocrisy, ultimately participating in a journey from exclusion to full inclusion and emotional bonding.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Squints reinforces group hierarchy by mocking Scotty's ignorance, positioning himself as a gatekeeper of baseball culture.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Squints fully integrates into the group through shared rituals and vulnerability, having experienced both judgment and acceptance.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: His initial role in alienating Scotty is never directly reconciled, leaving a quiet undercurrent of past judgment.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Romantic Initiation and the Cost of Courage
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Squints' infatuation with Wendy Peffercorn drives a personal arc of romantic ambition, culminating in a bold act that grants him legendary status but carries lasting consequences.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Squints is emotionally transformed by his first sight of Wendy, introducing a new personal goal beyond baseball.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He achieves his goal of kissing the lifeguard, gaining peer admiration but also a permanent ban from the pool and unresolved questions about consent and consequence.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The long-term emotional and social impact of his actions remains ambiguous, particularly regarding Wendy's perception and his own maturity.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Command Under Pressure: Leadership in the Retrieval Mission
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Squints transitions from a supportive role to a tactical leader during the high-stakes mission to retrieve the baseball, facing escalating risks and moments of personal vulnerability.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: The group is paralyzed by fear and lack of direction in retrieving the baseball.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Squints assumes command, directs the operation, and proposes a new tunneling strategy after a near-miss, demonstrating growth in leadership under pressure.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: His hesitation during the trap detonation raises doubts about his composure in crisis, suggesting lingering insecurity beneath his leadership role.
